description: A Chichewa-language government newspaper published by the Malawi Department of Information. Boma Lathu, meaning 'Our Government', covers a wide range of national topics including governance, agriculture, health, education, environment, gender, religion, economy, politics, sports, entertainment, poetry, fiction, and caricature, serving as an official public information resource for Malawian citizens.
themes: Governance, Agriculture, Health, Education, Environment, Gender, Religion, Economy, Politics, Sports, Entertainment, Poetry, Fiction

The collection documents official government engagements and public discourse on political and general issues across Malawi.

name: High Frequency Phone Survey
description: A household phone survey conducted across Malawi, covering multi-topic and multi-sectoral wellbeing indicators. The survey monitors key dimensions of household welfare at high frequency to support crisis preparedness and real-time response to large-scale shocks. The sample is representative both nationally and across the urban/rural divide. This is Round 1 of the survey, which is conducted in multiple rounds.
themes: Employment, Food Security, Food Insecurity Experience Scale, Household Businesses, Health, Shocks and Coping, Economics, Energy

themes: Employment, Food Security, Food Insecurity Experience Scale, Household Businesses, Health, Education, Shocks and Coping, Economics, Energy, Mental Health
